title: Credentials in proxy URL produce cryptic DNS error
description:
When a user passes a proxy URL with embedded credentials (e.g. socks5://user:pass@127.0.0.1:9050), parse_proxy_addr uses rsplit_once(':') to find the port. This correctly extracts port=9050, but the host portion becomes 'user:pass@127.0.0.1'.

This credential-tainted host string is passed directly to Socks5Stream::connect as the proxy address. tokio-socks calls to_socket_addrs() on ('user:pass@127.0.0.1', 9050), which either fails with a DNS resolution error (name not found) or resolves unexpectedly. The user sees a cryptic network error with no indication that credentials-in-URL is the problem.

The existing test (proxy_addr_parses_port_ignores_credentials) checks only that port==9050 is extracted correctly and notes in a comment 'tokio-socks will fail at connect time'. This is true but does not help the user debug.

The preferred fix is to detect '@' in the addr string after scheme stripping and return an explicit error: 'proxy URL credentials are not supported; use connect_with_password API or omit credentials'. If credential support is desired in future, parse and forward them to Socks5Stream::connect_with_password. The test comment should be updated to match whichever behavior is chosen.
